Container.Name: emptyarr
Container.Overview:
Safely empties Plex library trash by validating mount health before acting. Supports multiple Plex instances, mixed physical/debrid libraries, per-library cron schedules, dry runs, and Discord notifications.
Requires a config.yml — copy config.yml.example from the repo, fill in your Plex instances and library paths, then map it to /app/data/config.yml. Set your Plex tokens and API keys as environment variables below.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Port for the emptyarr web interface.

Config.Name: Config File Path
Config.Description: Full path to your config.yml file on the host. IMPORTANT: this file must exist before starting the container — if it does not exist Docker will create a directory here instead. Copy config.yml.example from the repo and fill it in first.

Config.Name: Debrid Symlink Path 1 (optional)
Config.Description: Host path to your debrid symlink movie library. Must match the container path declared in config.yml. Leave blank if not using debrid symlinks for movies.

Config.Name: Debrid Symlink Path 2 (optional)
Config.Description: Host path to your debrid symlink TV library. Must match the container path declared in config.yml. Leave blank if not using debrid symlinks for TV.

Config.Name: Physical Media Path 1 (optional)
Config.Description: Host path to your physical movie files. Must match the container path declared in config.yml.

Config.Name: Physical Media Path 2 (optional)
Config.Description: Host path to your physical TV files. Must match the container path declared in config.yml.

Config.Name: Debrid Mount Path (optional)
Config.Description: Your rclone/debrid mount path. Required if using debrid libraries — symlink targets must resolve inside the container. The container path must exactly match what your symlinks reference. Example: if symlinks point to /mnt/symlink_media/decypharr/mount/... then map that host path to the same container path.

Config.Name: Plex Token (Instance 1)
Config.Description: Plex token for your first Plex instance. The variable name must match your instance name in config.yml — uppercased with spaces/hyphens replaced by underscores. Example: instance named 'My Plex' uses PLEX_TOKEN_MY_PLEX. Find your token at: https://support.plex.tv/articles/204059436

Config.Name: Plex Token (Instance 2)
Config.Description: Plex token for your second Plex instance (if you have one). Rename this variable to match your second instance name in config.yml.

Config.Name: Real-Debrid API Key (optional)
Config.Description: Real-Debrid API key for provider health checks. Optional — leave blank to skip. Get yours at: https://real-debrid.com/apitoken

Config.Name: AllDebrid API Key (optional)
Config.Description: AllDebrid API key for provider health checks. Optional — leave blank to skip.

Config.Name: Torbox API Key (optional)
Config.Description: Torbox API key for provider health checks. Optional — leave blank to skip.

Config.Name: Debrid-Link API Key (optional)
Config.Description: Debrid-Link API key for provider health checks. Optional — leave blank to skip.

Config.Name: Discord Webhook URL (optional)
Config.Description: Discord webhook URL for failure/skip/success notifications. Optional — leave blank to disable. Create a webhook in your Discord server settings.

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Your local timezone for accurate cron scheduling and timestamps. Examples: America/New_York, America/Denver, America/Los_Angeles, Europe/London

Config.Name: Log Level
Config.Description: Logging verbosity. DEBUG shows all detail, INFO is normal, WARNING/ERROR for quiet operation.
